target_cmd: curl -s https://www.solaceagi.com | grep -E -o '(Download|Get Started|Try Free|Sign up|Start Free)' | wc -l | python3 -c "import sys; n=int(sys.stdin.read().strip()); print('CTA_COUNT:', n, '- STRONG' if n >= 2 else 'CTA_COUNT:', n, '- WEAK')"
target_name: Bruce Lee Attack — Homepage CTA Count (One Devastating Blow)
context: Bruce Lee oracle (q-agi-054): 'I fear not the man who has practiced 10,000 kicks once, but I fear the man who has practiced one kick 10,000 times.' Homepage has 3 CTA mentions (Download/Get Started/Try Free). This PASSES — there IS a primary CTA. Bruce Lee's deeper question: is there ONE primary CTA that dominates visually, or are 3 CTAs splitting user attention? Too many CTAs = scattered force. The ideal: one devastating blow (Download) supported by secondary CTAs.
